frame: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices and Home Cooking
generated_comment:
Vikram: Wah, aai ne proper Sunday lunch bana diya yaar, full ghar jaisa feel aa gaya.
Father: Haan, teri aai has always managed all this.
Vikram: Sahi mein. Tabhi bolta hoon, ghar ka khana aur ghar sambhalna alag hi cheez hoti hai... aajkal ki girls ko woh patience kahaan.
Father: Samjha, samjha. Biwi se kya expect hai, woh samajh gaya main.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because Vikram idealizes his mother's unpaid domestic labor and contrasts it with 'aajkal ki girls,' implying wives should cook and manage the home. It encodes a covert gender-role expectation through praise of maternal sacrifice and home cooking.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 34. Married two years ago. A man in Pune. Your wife Neha does not cook very often. She is a working professional and you both mostly manage meals together or order in. You are at your parents' house for the weekend. Your mother has cooked a full meal. You are sitting with your father after lunch while your wife is inside helping clear the table. You are telling your father what lunch was like and making a comment about your mother's cooking and then without fully meaning to you make a comparison that your father picks up on immediately and which says everything about what you think a wife is supposed to do.
